content.claim: Hungary shows extreme socio-economic educational inequality, with a 170-point reading comprehension gap between the top and bottom 10% socio-economically—equivalent to four years of formal education—and only 12.1% of disadvantaged students reaching good level in at least one basic skill, considerably below EU average.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Establishes current inequality baseline but does not isolate contribution of 6-/8-year gimnázium structure versus underlying socio-economic stratification
EU data at 2025 reporting standard may include revisions
Does not differentiate between school-type inequality effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
